commit.subject: [dawn][emscripten] Various future fixes for MLDrift.
commit.message:
[dawn][emscripten] Various future fixes for MLDrift.

- Allows passing a descriptor to createInstance, though it will
  currently be ignored.
- Reverts some changes from
  https://dawn-review.googlesource.com/c/dawn/+/217094
  that causes issues when building with ASYNCIFY instead of JSPI.
- Introduces a null Instance for now to help ease users that
  are injecting the Device. See the bug for more details.

No-Try: true
Bug: 388914937
Change-Id: I5ac816128e341436df4c50c21759ab19c5cc4dfb

commit.subject: [emscripten] Use handleAsync correctly in WaitAny
commit.message:
[emscripten] Use handleAsync correctly in WaitAny

I totally misunderstood how to use handleAsync before; what we had
worked for JSPI but not at all for old Asyncify (-sASYNCIFY=1).
This works in both.

In Asyncify, emwgpuWaitAny actually gets called twice. I suspect this is
because of how Asyncify works. (IIUC, each async point in wasm has a
pause/resume point where it can exit the stack and resume; it would make
sense that this cut point needs to pass through the JS stack too.) So
with the old usage of handleAsync, it was a total coincidence that
things worked fine. (In JSPI, async is handled natively so this was not
a problem.)

Bug: none
No-Try: true
Change-Id: I1ce057d7b68aa0895f815edac52cf160684edf02

commit.subject: [emscripten] Use handleAsync correctly in WaitAny
commit.message:
[emscripten] Use handleAsync correctly in WaitAny

I totally misunderstood how to use handleAsync before; what we had
worked for JSPI but not at all for old Asyncify (-sASYNCIFY=1).
This works in both.

In Asyncify, emwgpuWaitAny actually gets called twice. I suspect this is
because of how Asyncify works. (IIUC, each async point in wasm has a
pause/resume point where it can exit the stack and resume; it would make
sense that this cut point needs to pass through the JS stack too.) So
with the old usage of handleAsync, it was a total coincidence that
things worked fine. (In JSPI, async is handled natively so this was not
a problem.) Anyway, handleAsync is what takes care of this, so the
*whole* function needs to be wrapped in handleAsync, like this. This
makes it work in both Asyncify and JSPI.

Bug: none
No-Try: true
Change-Id: I1ce057d7b68aa0895f815edac52cf160684edf02

commit.subject: [emscripten] Use handleAsync correctly in WaitAny
commit.message:
[emscripten] Use handleAsync correctly in WaitAny

I totally misunderstood how to use handleAsync before; what we had
worked for JSPI but not at all for old Asyncify (-sASYNCIFY=1).
This works in both.

With ASYNCIFY=1, emwgpuInstanceWaitAny actually gets called twice, once
to start (while suspending), and once during stack rewind (while
resuming). handleAsync must be wrapped around the entire method to
handle the states correctly:
https://github.com/emscripten-core/emscripten/blob/dde19fae5259ab53942c52d67cf3edb360bda28a/src/library_async.js#L315
(In ASYNCIFY=2 aka JSPI=1, async is handled natively, so
emwgpuInstanceWaitAny is called only once.)

Bug: none
No-Try: true
Change-Id: I1ce057d7b68aa0895f815edac52cf160684edf02

commit.subject: [spirv-reader][ir] Add a `CullDistance` builtin.
commit.message:
[spirv-reader][ir] Add a `CullDistance` builtin.

In order to support SPIR-V coming from GLSL we need an internal concept
of the `CullDistance` builtin. This CL adds the infrastructure for that
builtin.

Bug: 42250952
Change-Id: Ia68fe6736385e752047d4d942f10039ea27b74d3

commit.subject: Mark some enum entries as `@internal`
commit.message:
Mark some enum entries as `@internal`

The defs file allow marking things as `@internal` which removes the
parsing code and does not add them to the list of suggested spellings.

This CL updates the `in` and `out` address spaces along with the
`point_size` builtin to be `@internal`. A series of tests are removed
which are from either old SPIR-V transform code, or ways of writing WGSL
which we no longer generate.

Bug: 42250952
Change-Id: I2aba36123bf0745652d90be4fa336137ebdda420

commit.subject: [tint][ir][val] Split up unittests into multiple files
commit.message:
[tint][ir][val] Split up unittests into multiple files

Breaks up the tests in validator_test.cc into multipl files with
conceptually connected tests, i.e. 'builtins', getting their own
file. This helps get the size of the testing files to a couple of kLOC
each instead of previous ~11kLOC single file.

The actual implementation the test fixture and a variety of misc
tests are left in validator_test.cc.

This CL is primarily just moving existing code around, and not to be a
systematic reorganization/refactoring of the existing tests.

Fixes: 388064480

Change-Id: Ie33da1fc4813e23535e162a152159e94721f3a58

```
The name of the file: docs/tint/fuzzers.md
Insertions: 7, Deletions: 2.

@@ -265,10 +265,15 @@
 ### `tint_ir_fuzzer`
 
 `tint_ir_fuzzer` accepts binary [protocol
-buffer](https://protobuf.dev/) input and uses
+buffer](https://protobuf.dev/) inputs and uses
 [`libprotobuf-mutator`](https://github.com/google/libprotobuf-mutator)
 for mutating this binary format directly.
 
+The suffix `.tirb` is sometimes used for IR fuzz test case files,
+which helps our tooling infer the format. The fuzzer itself does not
+generate/depend on this suffix though, instead using the `libFuzzer`
+standard prefixes, i.e. `crash-...` and `slow-...`, when needed.
+
 The input protobuf is defined in
 [src/tint/utils/protos/ir_fuzz/ir_fuzz.proto](../../src/tint/utils/protos/ir_fuzz/ir_fuzz.proto)
 is a composite of two elements, a protobuf
@@ -358,7 +363,7 @@
 
 ##### ir_fuzz_as
 
-For generating binary test cases a rudimentary assembeler,
+For generating binary test cases a rudimentary assembler,
 `ir_fuzz_as`, has been implemented.
 
 It is primarily used for converting the Tint test shaders into a seed
```

parents.subject: Fix crash when calling `GetBindGroupLayout()` on null bindGroupLayout

commit.subject: D3D11: Do not use half UAV slots for maxUAVsPerStage.
commit.message:
D3D11: Do not use half UAV slots for maxUAVsPerStage.

The doc https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/direct3d11/overviews-direct3d-11-resources-limits
doesn't mention that D3D11_PS_CS_UAV_REGISTER_COUNT represents total
UAVs limit of both compute & fragment stages.

Bug: chromium:387968846
Change-Id: Ia54cdfc0e3e925de128d3815a8b7a245407d2fd2

commit.subject: [Spirv] Clamp frag depth using push constant values
commit.message:
[Spirv] Clamp frag depth using push constant values

Add a way to use pre-created push constant layout generated by
`PreparePushConstants` to find the min and max values used for
frag depth clamping.

Bug: 366291600

Change-Id: I8393c912e48804614fd9fce8c1c1e9228f5b4594

commit.subject: Bump golang.org/x/crypto from v0.14.0 to v0.31.0
commit.message:
Bump golang.org/x/crypto from v0.14.0 to v0.31.0

Versions of the crypto module before v0.31.0 contain a potential
vulnerability in ServerConfig.PublicKeyCallback. The new version
contains a mitigation.

Note that it appears that dawn uses the crypto module only in tooling
for its hash functions and does not use the affected type. This change
simply updates the dependency to satisfy automatic dependency checker
tooling.

See: https://pkg.go.dev/vuln/GO-2024-3321

This change was generated by running:
  go get golang.org/x/crypto@v0.31

Change-Id: I990c5cf1e58abeba863021df8f3ba7813e9c11b9

commit.subject: Bump golang.org/x/crypto from v0.14.0 to v0.31.0
commit.message:
Bump golang.org/x/crypto from v0.14.0 to v0.31.0

Versions of the crypto module before v0.31.0 contain a potential
vulnerability in ServerConfig.PublicKeyCallback. The new version
contains a mitigation.

Note that it appears that dawn uses the crypto module only in tooling
for its hash functions and does not use the affected type. This change
simply updates the dependency to satisfy automatic dependency checker
tooling.

See: https://pkg.go.dev/vuln/GO-2024-3321

This change was generated by running:
  go get golang.org/x/crypto@v0.31

Change-Id: I990c5cf1e58abeba863021df8f3ba7813e9c11b9

commit.subject: Use CIPD for CMake on Windows
commit.message:
Use CIPD for CMake on Windows

CIPD now provides windows-arm64 binaries for CMake, so we can use that
instead of downloading from Google storage.

This gets us a newer version of CMake, which is needed for the latest
version of SPIRV-Tools.

Fixed: 388504160
Change-Id: I1ecb7dceee0bbfd3aa9042cb88c09bd5516f550a

commit.subject: [tint][ir][val] Reject IR with abstract values
commit.message:
[tint][ir][val] Reject IR with abstract values

Abstract values should be removed at the resolve stage before IR is
generated, so should not be present when validating IR.

Fixes: 388286932
Change-Id: I0fec91d1a11ca0ec848234a15d77faf08b15bb57

commit.subject: webgpu.h: update WGPUBindGroupLayoutEntry and sentinels
commit.message:
webgpu.h: update WGPUBindGroupLayoutEntry and sentinels

This CL adds Undefined for the following binding types and change the
default value in binding layout for those:
- WGPUBufferBindingType: Undefined -> Uniform
- WGPUSamplerBindingType: Undefined -> Filtering
- WGPUTextureSampleType: Undefined -> Float
- WGPUStorageTextureAccess: Undefined -> WriteOnly

Spec PR: https://github.com/webgpu-native/webgpu-headers/pull/364

Bug: 377820810
Change-Id: Ic96a6d2cc1387b6ebe9cef9a9dd1aa2e6789529c

commit.subject: Validate UTF8 conversion is in range.
commit.message:
Validate UTF8 conversion is in range.

When converting UTF8 characters, we'd check the various bytes but we did
not validate that the resulting value was within range of the codepoints
supported by the number of bytes decoded.

This CL adds a check to the UTF-8 decoder to validate that the resulting
`uint32_t` value is within range for the number of source bytes.

Bug: 388039766
Change-Id: Ie7ed6a19e84f04356e3fdeba578281cf966145f6

commit.subject: Ignore empty bind group layout when creating pipeline layout
commit.message:
Ignore empty bind group layout when creating pipeline layout

According to the latest WebGPU SPEC, when creating pipeline layout, the
empty bind group layouts should all be treated as null bind group layouts
and be ignored when checking setBindGroup() against the current pipeline.

Bug: 377836524, 42241530
Test: dawn_unittests
Change-Id: I84dcaf522fac76a6eb61f86f82f6a8c93b2020eb

commit.message:
D3D12: Re-enable GPU_BASED_VALIDATION_INCOMPATIBLE_RESOURCE_STATE

The warning `GPU_BASED_VALIDATION_INCOMPATIBLE_RESOURCE_STATE` can be
enabled now as the related bug in D3D12 debug layer has been fixed in
the latest Windows SDK.

commit.subject: [tools] Fix format string errors
commit.message:
[tools] Fix format string errors

Fixes cases in Go code where Printf, Fprintf, Errorf, etc. were being
called with non-constant format strings. In most cases, this was due
to the wrong function being used since formatting was not actually
needed.

These errors appear to have been around for a long time, but a change
in the Go version or something caused these to start causing test
failures.

Change-Id: Ie329a939183d6274d99b945b0bedab4a3bcf8938

commit.subject: Update `InsertAfter` naming.
commit.message:
Update `InsertAfter` naming.

The name `InsertAfter` is confusing as it can receive a `FunctionParam`
or `BlockParam` as argument, but it inserts into the _block_ after those
instructions, not the instruction itself.

Update the name to `InsertInBlockAfter` to make it clearer where the
instructions will be placed.

Bug: 361142936
Change-Id: I5ae9b180e274bd6c0589d698a678571de7384347

commit.subject: Add `tools/run tests` flag for SPIR-V Ir Reader.
commit.message:
Add `tools/run tests` flag for SPIR-V Ir Reader.

This Cl adds a `--use-ir-reader` flag to the `tools/run tests` commands
which enables use of the new SPIR-V IR Reader in Tint.

commit.subject: Rename `kAllowPointersInStructures` capability.
commit.message:
Rename `kAllowPointersInStructures` capability.

The `kAllowPointersInStructures` has been expanded to mean both pointers
and items in the handle address space. This CL updates the name to be
`kAllowPointersAndHandlesInStructures` to indicate the expanded scope.

Bug: 370690268
Change-Id: I2c40951d8012895aad91c3963cda7292b3ad2b62

commit.subject: Compat: move new limit validation.
commit.message:
Compat: move new limit validation.

maxStorage(Buffer/Texture)sIn(Fragment/Vertex)Stage limits
where enforced at create pipeline time but have been moved
to create bind group and create pipeline layout time and
transitively still at create pipeline time when the pipeline
is using auto layout.

Note: that it is not entirely settled how the limits are
affected in relation to maxStorage(Buffer/Texture)sPerShaderStage.

Bug: 383157198,381214210
Change-Id: I752062e45eb470b90d2cd89e41474a55d9fd3c6d
